description_long: This object is a stylized green flower with broad, flat petals arranged in multiple layers radiating outward from the center. The petals are large, angular, and have visible vein patterns, giving them a realistic leaf-like appearance. The central part of the flower is slightly raised, with smaller, upright structures that mimic the look of stamens or inner petals. The overall form is low and wide, with the petals overlapping in a whorled, symmetrical pattern. There is no visible stem, and the flower appears to rest directly on a surface. The coloration is a uniform green, suggesting a decorative or artificial representation rather than a natural blooming flower.
description: A stylized green flower with broad, veined petals arranged in layered, symmetrical whorls and no visible stem. The flower is low, wide, and features a raised central area with upright inner structures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the layered, overlapping petals radiating outward and the raised central area with upright inner structures.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the petal layering and the slight elevation of the central part, with more visible depth between petal layers.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and whorled arrangement of the petals.
description_view_3: Side-angled view from the opposite direction, again showing the layered petals and the central raised area.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with multiple layers. Each layer overlaps the one beneath it, creating a symmetrical, circular pattern typical of many ornamental flowers.
